commands.review_summary:
The PR retunes aggregate publisher abuse scoring to a v4 model with a 200-skill label floor, sublinear adoption offsets, schema compatibility for old configs, focused tests, and updated moderation spec text.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection shows the v4 model-version bump writes and updates nominations under a new modelVersion, while the dashboard fallback can still read old pending v2 nominations by status and label.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can clear or filter stale old-model aggregate nominations and add regression coverage before this PR lands.

target.title: Tune publisher abuse pressure labels
target.body:
## Summary
- Moves aggregate publisher spam-abuse labels to a v4 model with a 200-skill maturity pivot.
- Below 200 skills, publishers can shape the cohort baseline but cannot receive aggregate spam reason codes, review labels, or potential-ban labels from this path.
- At or above 200 skills, catalog volume raises pressure, but installs, stars, and downloads now lower pressure using whole-publisher adoption signals that scale sublinearly with catalog size.
- Updates Convex finalization, schema compatibility, tests, and the internal moderation spec for the new rule.

## What Changed
| Area | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Minimum catalog size | 100-skill pivot, below-pivot publishers could still receive review labels | 200-skill pivot; below-pivot publishers always label `pass` for aggregate spam abuse |
| Cohort baseline | All non-zero-skill non-official publishers | Still all non-zero-skill non-official publishers, so the population baseline remains stable |
| Potential-ban label | High z-score plus prior maturity check | High z-score plus `publishedSkills >= 200` |
| Adoption signals | Per-skill installs/stars/downloads had to keep pace linearly with catalog size | Whole-publisher installs/stars/downloads lower pressure; engagement expectations grow sublinearly with catalog size |
| Old score runs | Model config had no `engagementElasticity` field | Schema accepts old configs; missing field falls back to the old linear behavior |

## Impact Check
Corrected live production re-score, using canonical all-time skill stats and excluding official publishers before cohort scoring:

| Model | Eligible publishers | Potential ban | Review | Pass |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| v4 200-skill model before adoption retune | 11 | 8 | 3 | 22,124 |
| v4 adoption-retuned model | 11 | 2 | 3 | 22,130 |

Known calibration examples:

| Publisher | Updated outcome | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ivangdavila` | pass | 955 skills, 84,756 all-time installs, 4,924 stars; adoption is strong enough to offset catalog size |
| `harrylabsj` | pass | 600 skills, 7,521 all-time installs, 17 stars; no longer auto-ban/review just for catalog size |
| `oomol` | potential-ban candidate | 582 skills, 4,153 all-time installs, 0 stars, low downloads per skill; org status is not a scoring bypass |
| `ai-gaoqian` | potential-ban candidate | 212 skills, 855 all-time installs, 5 stars, low downloads per skill |
| `justoneapi` | review | 224 skills, 3,164 all-time installs, 0 stars; corrected all-time installs keep it out of auto-ban |
| `spacesq` | pass | 63 skills, below the 200-skill spam-abuse label floor |
| `teoslayer` | pass | 174 skills, below the 200-skill spam-abuse label floor |

The raw production candidate rows stay local and are not included in this PR. Official publishers are excluded before scoring/finalization, so they do not contribute to cohort stats and cannot be nominated by this path.

## Proof
| Evidence | Claim Proved | How To Check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts` | Below-200 publishers are forced to `pass`; high-adoption bulk publishers like Ivan/Harry pass; low-adoption bulk publishers still get review/ban labels | `bun run test convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts` |
| `convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts` | Convex finalization writes below-200 high-z publishers as `pass` with z-score 0 and no nomination | `bun run test convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts` |
| `convex/schema.ts` | Stored old model configs remain valid because the new engagement field is optional | `bunx tsc --noEmit` |
| `specs/security-moderation.md` | Internal moderation intent matches the v4 scorer behavior | Read the Publisher abuse scoring section |

## Verification
```text
bun run test convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts
bun run format:check -- convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.ts convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts convex/schema.ts specs/security-moderation.md
bun run lint -- convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.ts convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts convex/schema.ts
bunx tsc --noEmit
bun run ci:static
```
target.author: jesse-merhi
